rationale: In the theory section, the citing paper explicitly summarizes Morales and Fitzsimons (2007) as showing that contact with a disgusting product contaminates a target product and lowers evaluations. The authors then say that, based on this contagion literature, they develop their understanding of art infusion as a special spillover effect. This is more than a passing background mention because the cited paper helps anchor the conceptual framing of how properties transfer across associated products.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Morales and Fitzsimons (2007) as prior evidence that product-to-product contact can transfer negative essence and depress evaluations. It leverages that finding to frame art infusion as a related spillover process in which properties of art transfer to associated products.
